mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market reprices equity valuations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.04% suggests accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates below neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 3.7% indicates tight labor market conditions that sustain wage growth and consumer spending power. Persistently low unemployment drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.5% is historically tight
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when UNRATE < 3.7% do not exceed median returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% by at least 150 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 8% month-over-month, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This negative demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: demand-sensitive regime with cyclical sensitivity to housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following HOUST declines > 8%, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 110 while unemployment remains below 3.7%, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization combined with tight labor markets. This dual tightness signals inflation pressure that forces Fed tightening, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.92 near threshold, UNRATE at 3.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with supply constraints and tight labor
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both INDPRO > 110 and UNRATE < 3.7%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while housing starts remain above 1.3 million units annualized, indicating synchronized demand strength across consumer and residential sectors. This broad-based demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 526057 million, HOUST at 1364 thousand
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns when both conditions met do not exceed baseline by at least 200 basis points, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month while unemployment rises above 3.8%, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes broader economic slowdown. This labor market weakness produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 151722 thousand, UNRATE at 3.5%
regime_hint: early-stage labor market deterioration signaling cyclical downturn
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both PAYEMS growth < 100k and UNRATE > 3.8%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation rises above 2.8% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative despite inflation acceleration. This policy lag produces positive equity returns over 5 trading days as markets price in continued liquidity support.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions met, or interaction effect shows negative coefficient (opposite of predicted direction).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) show month-over-month growth above 0.5%, indicating sustained demand momentum. This persistent demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings guidance remains constructive.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19021.86 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 526057 million
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 > 2.5% growth do not exceed median returns by at least 100 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
